subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Rate & Accumulation (Escalator Line)
stimulus:
People enter a line for an escalator at a rate modeled by the function $r$ given by
$$r(t) = \begin{cases} 44\left(\dfrac{t}{100}\right)^3\left(1 - \dfrac{t}{300}\right)^7 & \text{for } 0 \leq t \leq 300 \\ 0 & \text{for } t > 300, \end{cases}$$
where $r(t)$ is measured in people per second and $t$ is measured in seconds. As people get on the escalator, they exit the line at a constant rate of $0.7$ person per second. There are $20$ people in line at time $t = 0$.

parts.prompt: How many people enter the line for the escalator during the time interval $0 \leq t \leq 300$? Give your answer to the nearest whole number.

scoring_points.description: Sets up the correct integral: $\int_0^{300} r(t)\,dt$
Correct answer: $\int_0^{300} r(t)\,dt \approx 270.031$, so approximately $270$ people enter the line

parts.prompt: During the time interval $0 \leq t \leq 300$, there are always people in line for the escalator. How many people are in line at time $t = 300$? Give your answer to the nearest whole number.

scoring_points.description: Sets up the correct expression: $20 + \int_0^{300} r(t)\,dt - 0.7(300)$, representing the initial $20$ people plus those who entered minus those who exited at the constant rate of $0.7$ per second
Correct answer: $20 + 270.031 - 210 \approx 80.031$, so approximately $80$ people are in line at $t = 300$

parts.prompt: For $t > 300$, what is the first time $t$ at which there are no people in line for the escalator? Give your answer to the nearest whole number.

scoring_points.description: For $t > 300$, $r(t) = 0$, so people only exit at rate $0.7$ per second. The number of people in line at time $t > 300$ is $L(300) - 0.7(t - 300)$. Setting this equal to $0$: $t = 300 + \dfrac{L(300)}{0.7} \approx 300 + \dfrac{80.031}{0.7} \approx 414.330$, so $t \approx 414$ seconds.

parts.prompt: For $0 \leq t \leq 300$, at what time $t$ is the number of people in line a minimum? To the nearest whole number, find the number of people in line at this time. Justify your answer.

scoring_points.description: Identifies that the number of people in line changes at rate $r(t) - 0.7$. Sets $r(t) - 0.7 = 0$ to find critical point(s), or equivalently $r(t) = 0.7$.
Finds the critical point at $t \approx 33.108$ seconds (where $r(t) = 0.7$ for the first time, as $r$ is increasing through $0.7$). Considers endpoints $t = 0$ and $t = 300$ as well.

Justification that the minimum occurs at $t \approx 33.108$: For $0 < t < 33.108$, $r(t) < 0.7$ so the number of people is decreasing; for $33.108 < t \leq 300$, $r(t) > 0.7$ so the number is increasing. Therefore the minimum occurs at $t \approx 33.108$.

required_elements: $r(t) < 0.7$ for $0 < t < 33.108$ (line decreasing) and $r(t) > 0.7$ for $33.108 < t < 300$ (line increasing), confirming minimum at $t \approx 33.108$

scoring_points.description: Correct minimum number of people: $L(t^*) = 20 + \int_0^{t^*}\bigl(r(t) - 0.7\bigr)\,dt \approx 20 - 6.370 \approx 14$ people (to the nearest whole number)

required_elements: $14$ people in line at the minimum
